seo.title: Wan 2.5 – Text or Image to Video with Optional Audio (480p–1080p, 5–10s)
seo.description: Audio-ready 5–10s clips at 480p/720p/1080p. Text or image input, optional WAV/MP3 upload, native audio, prompt expansion toggle, and pay-as-you-go pricing in MaxVideoAI.

overview: Wan 2.5 in MaxVideoAI: audio-ready 5–10s clips from text or a single image, at 480p/720p/1080p. Optional WAV/MP3 upload, native audio when no track is provided, prompt expansion toggle, and per-second pricing by resolution.

hero.title: Wan 2.5 – Text or Image to Video with Optional Audio in MaxVideoAI (480p–1080p, 5–10s)
hero.intro: Audio-ready cinematic beats from text or a still — sync to your track or let Wan generate sound.
hero.badge: 480p/720p/1080p · 5–10s · Text or Image input · Optional audio

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video; Image → Video from a single still

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio if no track; optional WAV/MP3 upload (3–30 s, ~15 MB) trimmed/looped to 5 or 10 s

technicalOverview.label: Controls
technicalOverview.body: Prompt expansion toggle; optional seed; safety toggles

promptStructure.title: Text-to-Video with Wan 2.5
promptStructure.quote: [Duration] second [aspect ratio] cinematic shot of [subject] in [environment]. Camera [movement] while [main action] happens. Lighting [style], [grade] look. Audio: [ambience + music/SFX], optional dialogue: “[…]”.
promptStructure.description: Write prompts like a shot description plus sound notes: subject/tone, environment, camera, timing, lighting/look, and audio cues.
steps: Camera language (dolly, pan, crane, POV, orbit)
Lighting and look (golden hour, moody, neon, etc.)
Audio: ambience, SFX, music, optional short dialogue

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Attach a trimmed 5–10s WAV/MP3 when you need visuals synced to beats
Use native audio (no upload) for quick ideation
Toggle Prompt expansion on for exploration, off for precise briefs
Iterate cheap at 480p, finish at 1080p once approved
Keep copy as overlays; tiny text/UI can be unreliable

prompts.title: Chrome concept car on downbeats (10s, 16:9)
prompts.prompt: 10s 16:9 cinematic shot of a chrome concept car emerging from foggy light pulses on synth downbeats. Wide to low-angle push, rain streaks, cool blue rim light, subtle flares. Audio: uploaded track as main music, add low rumble, thunder, raindrops, no dialogue.

prompts.title: Vertical fitness watch story (10s, 9:16)
prompts.prompt: 10s vertical shot of a fitness smartwatch on a runner’s wrist synced to an energetic electronic track: close-up on beat one with raindrops on glass; pull back on the first big hit as the runner sprints in slow motion on a neon-lit bridge; final beat swings to profile close-up with breath in cold air, watch display glowing. Audio: uploaded track + subtle footsteps, rain, breathing; no VO.

faqs.a: Yes. If you don’t upload a track, Wan generates native audio. If you upload WAV/MP3, your track is trimmed/looped to 5 or 10 seconds and used as the main audio.

faqs.q: What resolutions and durations should I use?
faqs.a: 480p/5s for fastest look-dev; 720p/5–10s for internal reviews and social; 1080p/10s for hero beats and client-ready shots.

faqs.q: Can Wan 2.5 handle vertical and square videos?
faqs.a: Yes. Choose 16:9, 9:16 or 1:1 before rendering; 9:16 is best for mobile-first placements.
Yes. Upload one still (portrait, product, concept art) and focus the prompt on motion, camera and audio.

faqs.q: How is Wan 2.5 priced versus other engines?
faqs.a: Per-second by resolution (0.05/0.10/0.15 $/s). It’s mid-tier: cheaper than premium long-form, more capable than ultra-budget silent engines.

custom.heroDesc1: Wan 2.5 lets you storyboard cinematic beats with built-in audio: prompt or image input, optional WAV/MP3, 5 or 10 seconds at 480p/720p/1080p.
custom.heroDesc2: Use it for hero beats and reveals where music, ambience or dialogue matter. Upload a track for tight sync or let Wan score the scene natively.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for music-led reveals, voiceover hooks, and punchy hero beats that feel finished.
heroHighlights: Audio + visuals together||Native dialogue, ambience, and SFX in the same render.
Bring your own track||Use an audio URL/upload to lock timing to music or VO.
Stronger prompt follow-through||Camera + sound cues land more consistently with structured direction.
Style-flexible outputs||Works for both realistic and stylized looks when you keep prompts concrete.
custom.whatTitle: What Wan 2.5 Actually Is in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Wan 2.5 is a text-and-image-to-video model built for short cinematic clips with native audio.
custom.whatIntro2: In MaxVideoAI it’s a flexible, audio-ready engine with tiered pricing by resolution.
whatFlowSteps: 2. Choose Text → Video or Image → Video.
3. Set duration (5/10 s), resolution (480p/720p/1080p) and aspect ratio.
5. Decide if Prompt expansion stays on.
6. Paste a clear, cinematic prompt with subject, camera and sound.
custom.specNote: These specs describe Wan 2.5 exactly as you can use it today via MaxVideoAI.

specSections.title: Audio-led workflows
specSections.intro: Designed for sound-led clips where timing matters. Use it to sync visuals to music or voiceover.
items: Add music or VO cues in the prompt.
Use an audio URL to lock timing.
Keep visuals aligned with the beat.

specSections.title: Prompt discipline
specSections.intro: Structured direction yields more reliable results than long prose. Keep instructions clear and sequential.
items: Reserve complex styling for later passes.

custom.specValueProp: Wan 2.5 is the audio-ready short-form engine for 5–10s beats where visuals and sound need to land together.
custom.galleryIntro: Clips generated with the exact configuration you have access to in MaxVideoAI.
custom.imageIntro: Animate a single still into an audio-backed beat.
imageFlow: Upload a portrait, product shot or concept art still.
Choose Image → Video, duration, resolution and aspect ratio.
Attach audio or let Wan generate it.
Prompt for motion (camera/subject) and how the beat ends at 5 or 10 s.
imageWhy: Concept art turned into audio-backed intro beats
custom.multishotIntro1: Wan 2.5 can tie visual movement to a specific track.
custom.multishotIntro2: Use downbeats and transitions as anchors in your prompt.
multishotTips: Trim audio to 5 or 10 s for precise beat placement
Call out when visuals should hit specific beats
Keep dialogue short and natural within 5–10 s
Draft with native audio; upload polished tracks for finals
strengths: Treat it as a 5–10s “hero beat”: one subject, one clear action, one camera move.
If timing matters, use an Audio URL and describe what should land on key moments (1–2 cues max).
Keep dialogue short (one line). Ambience + one SFX cue is usually enough.
For Image→Video, start from a clean still and prompt motion + camera — don’t re-describe the whole scene.
Prompt expansion is great for short prompts; keep your input literal and structured so it expands in the right direction.
boundaries: Audio URL: if audio is longer than the video, it’s truncated; if shorter, the remaining video is silent.
Prompts are short-form (max ~800 chars). Negative prompts are capped too — keep them minimal.
Safety checks can block borderline content — keep people/likeness and dialogue brand-safe.
safetyRules: No sexual content, and nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or graphic-violence content.
Don’t impersonate real people or public figures; use consent for any likeness/voice.
Don’t upload private personal data or copyrighted material you don’t have rights to (including audio).
Some prompts, images, or audio may be blocked by safety filters.
comparisonPoints: Wan 2.5: audio-ready 5–10s beats with optional track upload and flexible resolution tiers
Sora 2: 720p with native audio for realistic UGC/product shots when you want OpenAI-style motion
Veo 3.1 / Kling / Pika: pick these for framing presets, silent 1080p realism, or stylized loops

faqs.a: Yes. Native audio if no track is uploaded; uploaded WAV/MP3 becomes the main soundtrack, trimmed or looped to 5/10 s.

faqs.q: What resolutions and durations should I use?
faqs.a: 480p/5s for fastest and cheapest tests; 720p/5–10s for internal and social; 1080p/10s for hero beats and client delivery.

faqs.q: Can Wan 2.5 handle vertical and square videos?
faqs.a: Yes—16:9, 9:16 or 1:1. Pick 9:16 for mobile-first placements.
Yes. Upload one still and focus the prompt on motion, camera and audio.

faqs.q: How is Wan 2.5 priced versus other engines?
faqs.a: Per-second by resolution (0.05/0.10/0.15 $/s); mid-tier compared to premium long-form and ultra-budget silent engines.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare price, latency and output options across the MaxVideoAI catalog.
custom.finalPara1: Wan 2.5 in MaxVideoAI is your audio-ready short-form engine for 5–10 second beats.
custom.finalPara2: Use native or uploaded audio, iterate cheap, and finish in HD when visuals and sound must land together.
custom.tipsIntro: Wan 2.5 works best for short, sound-led beats — keep the visual brief simple and let timing come from the audio.
troubleshootingItems: Audio feels off → remember uploaded audio is trimmed to the first 5/10s; prompt to the segment you’re actually using.
Too much happening / messy motion → cut to one main action; remove extra beats; simplify the background.
Drift / ignores details → move subject + action + camera to the first line; keep constraints positive (“clean background”, “centered subject”).
Lip sync drifts → shorten the line and slow the delivery; avoid long monologues.
Prompt expansion changes nuance → disable expansion for literal control, or shorten the prompt and remove ambiguous adjectives.

hero.title: Wan 2.5
hero.subtitle: Audio-ready 5-10s clips for text or image starts, prompt expansion, and 480p to 1080p checks.
hero.paragraph: Use Wan 2.5 when you need the supported older Wan route for short audio-ready tests: text-to-video, image-to-video, optional soundtrack upload, prompt expansion, seed control and lower-resolution draft passes.

decisionCards.title: Wan 2.5 or Wan 2.6?
decisionCards.body: Use Wan 2.5 for short audio-ready checks and lower-resolution drafts. Use Wan 2.6 when you need 15s, multi-shot or reference-video guidance.

decisionCards.title: Need the soundtrack to steer timing?
decisionCards.body: Attach a short audio file when rhythm or mood should guide the clip, then keep the visual prompt simple.

decisionCards.title: Comparing audio-native routes?
decisionCards.body: Compare Wan 2.5 with Sora 2 when you are choosing between lower-cost checks and Sora-style synced outputs.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text prompt
referenceWorkflows.body: Describe one subject, one action, one camera move and one sound direction.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image start
referenceWorkflows.body: Use a single image to hold framing, product shape or character identity.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio file
referenceWorkflows.body: Attach a short soundtrack when rhythm, ambience or mood should drive the take.

referenceWorkflows.title: Prompt expansion
referenceWorkflows.body: Turn expansion on for sparse briefs; turn it off when every visual detail is deliberate.

referenceWorkflows.title: Wan 2.6 upgrade
referenceWorkflows.body: Move to Wan 2.6 for longer clips, multi-shot plans or reference-video consistency.

section.title: How to Write a Great Wan 2.5 Prompt
section.intro: Wan 2.5 works best with a single clear action and a short, concrete prompt.
section.tip: Tip: duration + aspect ratio are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, motion, camera, lighting, style, and optional sound. Prompt expansion helps short prompts.

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.
tabs.copy:
[Subject] [action] in [scene], [camera move], [lighting/style], [optional sound cue].
Negative: [text, logos, extra people, blur]

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
tabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + one action.]

Scene:
[Location + time + 2 anchors.]

Camera:
[Shot + angle + one move.]

Look:
[Lighting + palette + style.]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1-2 cues.]

Negative:
[What to avoid.]

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "studio brief")
tabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific look or continuity across shots.
tabs.copy:
Brief:
[Goal + vibe.]

Subject + setting:
[Key traits + location + anchors.]

Camera:
[Framing + angle + one move + lens feel.]

Lighting + color:
[Key light + palette.]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1-2 cues.]

Negative:
[What to avoid.]

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = style control. Use for precise, repeatable looks.

globalPrinciples: Use a short negative prompt to avoid artifacts.
engineWhy: Audio URL can sync timing; keep cues minimal.
Avoid multiple beats in one short clip.

demo.title: Demo: a prompt for Wan 2.5
demo.prompt:
Subject: Fitness smartwatch on a runner’s wrist
Action: Shot follows the run and beat changes of the track
Camera: Close-up, then synchronized pull-back
Style: Vertical product sport story, rain and music energy
Audio: Energetic electronic track
